content: bd create uses --description or -d for the description flag, NOT --desc. Title is a positional arg: bd create 'title' --description 'desc' --priority P2

agent-busy marker pattern: .legio/agent-busy/{agentName} file presence indicates agent is actively executing a tool. Absence means idle. Used by mail auto-nudge smart push delivery to decide whether to call nudgeAgent() directly or only write pending marker.

Smart push delivery in mail send: when shouldNudge is true, always write pending nudge marker first (reliable path), then check isAgentIdle(). If idle, call nudgeAgent(cwd, to, '[mail from X] reason: subject', true) with force=true to skip debounce. nudgeAgent is non-fatal (returns delivered:false on failure, never throws).

bin/legio.mjs shim: use register('tsx/esm', import.meta.url) not register('tsx/esm', pathToFileURL('./')) — pathToFileURL('./') resolves relative to CWD which breaks global installs; import.meta.url is always the shim file's own URL

Agent busy-state tracking pattern in log.ts: tool-start creates .legio/agent-busy/{agentName} file (non-fatal try/catch), tool-end removes it (non-fatal, file may not exist). Used by watchdog/monitor to detect in-flight tool executions without querying SQLite.

waitForTuiReady pattern: polls tmux capture-pane -p until pane has non-empty content, replaces hardcoded sleep(3000) before sendKeys beacon. Default timeout 15s, interval 500ms. Returns Promise<void>, warns stderr on timeout but never throws. Callers proceed regardless.

sendKeys error differentiation: parse stderr for 'no server running' (AgentError: Tmux server not running) vs 'session not found'/'can't find session' (AgentError: Session not found) vs other (generic AgentError with stderr). Callers pattern-match on err.message.

Commit messages containing 'git push' (the two words together) trigger the PreToolUse Bash danger guard, blocking the commit. Write commit messages that avoid 'git push' literally — use 'remote-block', 'push to remote', or similar phrasing instead.

hooks.json.tmpl push-guard pattern: the PreToolUse Bash entry uses ENV_GUARD + read stdin + sed extraction of command field + grep -qE for git push pattern. Does NOT use {{AGENT_NAME}} — agent name not needed for simple push blocking.

PostToolUse mulch diff hook pattern: matcher 'Bash', reads stdin, extracts command field via sed, checks grep -qE for git commit pattern, then runs 'mulch diff HEAD~1' to show what changed in the commit. Enables automatic diff capture after each commit.

TaskDetailView route pattern: #task/:taskId uses parseHash() which splits on '/', so param=taskId. Router case 'task' passes taskId prop to TaskDetailView. Reached via IssueCard onClick (not NAV_LINKS).

IssueCard click-through change: onClick now sets location.hash = 'task/' + issue.id (not '#task/...' — hash prefix is optional in assignment). Removed appState/pendingChatContext import as it was the only use.

zombie reconciliation fix: do NOT call updateLastActivity() after updateState('zombie') in coordinator.ts and gateway.ts — the SQL CASE in updateLastActivity transitions zombie->working, immediately overriding the state. Only call updateState when marking zombie; skip updateLastActivity in that code path.

inferDomainsFromFiles() in src/mulch/client.ts maps file paths to mulch domains using glob patterns. DEFAULT_DOMAIN_MAP maps src/commands/**->cli, src/server/routes*->server, src/server/public/**->frontend, src/sessions/**->server, src/metrics/**->server, src/events/**->server, src/mail/**->server, src/agents/**->swarm, src/worktree/**->swarm+merge, src/merge/**->merge, src/beads/**->cli, src/*.test.*->testing, src/**/*.test.*->testing, agents/**->swarm, templates/**->swarm. sling.ts calls inferDomainsFromFiles(fileScope) and passes inferred domains to overlay mulchDomains and mulch.prime().

LegioConfig.mulch.domainMap is an optional Record<string, string[]> for custom file-glob-to-domain mapping. When empty or absent, inferDomainsFromFiles falls back to DEFAULT_DOMAIN_MAP. Config default is {} (empty object). Made optional (not required) to avoid breaking existing test fixtures that construct LegioConfig without domainMap.

inferDomainsFromFiles pattern: DEFAULT_DOMAIN_MAP (exported) maps glob patterns to domain arrays; matchGlob() (internal) converts glob to regex handling ** (zero-or-more segments) and * (single-segment); inferDomainsFromFiles(files, domainMap?) returns deduplicated domain array. All three live in src/mulch/client.ts above createMulchClient.

sling domain inference pattern: compute inferredDomains from fileScope before the mulchExpertise block; pass inferredDomains to mulch.prime() when non-empty (else fall back to --files); use inferredDomains for overlayConfig.mulchDomains when non-empty (else fall back to config.mulch.domains). Avoids double-call by computing once and reusing.

stdout-before-beacon pattern: gateway.ts, coordinator.ts, supervisor.ts must write JSON/text output to stdout immediately after store.upsert(), BEFORE the blocking beacon sleep+sendKeys operations. When callers pipe stdout (e.g. runLegio), tmux child inherits the pipe FD so sleeping first keeps the pipe open and hangs the caller.

coordinator.ts early JSON output uses watchdog:false, monitor:false defaults since output is written before those operations complete. Non-JSON supplementary messages (watchdog/monitor PID) remain in-place after their operations.
supervisor.ts store.upsert() must happen BEFORE beacon sendKeys (same as coordinator/gateway pattern) so hook-triggered updateLastActivity() can find the session entry.

parseTranscriptTexts in transcript.ts uses a line-offset watermark (nextLine) for incremental JSONL parsing. Trailing empty from split('\n') is excluded from nextLine: nextLine = lastLine.trim().length === 0 ? lines.length - 1 : lines.length.

session-end chat sync pattern for persistent agents: resolve transcript via .transcript-path cache, read .chat-transcript-offset watermark, call parseTranscriptTexts(path, fromLine), filter to assistant messages, insert into chat.db coordinator session via createChatStore, then writeFile offset. Non-fatal try-catch wraps entire block.

POST /api/coordinator/chat persists user messages to SQLite (chat.db), forwards to coordinator via sendKeys with newline-flattening (→ space) to prevent embedded Enter in Claude Code TUI input handling

Daemon spawn env hygiene: when server.ts spawns a detached daemon, strip __LEGIO_TSX_LOADED from child env (const childEnv: NodeJS.ProcessEnv = { ...process.env, LEGIO_SERVER_DAEMON: '1' }; delete childEnv.__LEGIO_TSX_LOADED) so bin/legio.mjs re-execs with --import tsx. On Node v23+, the shim cannot strip types without tsx active, but children inherit the parent env var and skip re-exec.

bin/legio.mjs shim hardening for node_modules: check process.execArgv for '--import tsx' in addition to __LEGIO_TSX_LOADED. When inNodeModules=true, only skip re-exec when tsxImportActive is also true. This prevents ERR_UNSUPPORTED_NODE_MODULES_TYPE_STRIPPING on Node v23+ when daemon children inherit __LEGIO_TSX_LOADED=1 but tsx is not active.
